model.draft_attempted: Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct-int4 (nearest same-tokenizer draft; 0.5B variant not on disk)

measurement_protocol.report: median + min + max + p95 + stddev (raw per-run values in runs/<ts>/raw.json)
measurement_protocol.warmup_purpose: two throwaway requests after server start; primes the model and Metal kernels so steady-state numbers are measured
measurement_protocol.tie_threshold: Winner column awarded only when delta exceeds 5%; otherwise reported as tie.

phase0_preflight.description: Probe every v4 feature for actual functionality before measuring.

phase1_daemon_warm_wall.description: Time from HTTP request send to first streamed token, model already resident.
phase1_daemon_warm_wall.note: The v4 RESULTS.md called this 'cold wall' but the spec is wall-time to first token with the daemon already running; that is, by definition, warm TTFT. We measure both 'fresh prompt' (different content each run) and 'repeated prompt' (cache-hit eligible) variants.
phase1_daemon_warm_wall.what_was_skipped: squishd UDS daemon: the new socket daemon does not load mlx-native quantized models — it crashes looking for '<model>-compressed/manifest.json'. We fall back to the HTTP-based 'squish daemon start' (which is just 'python -m squish.server' kept alive). See PRECHECK.md.

phase2_kv_cache_ttft.description: TTFT delta between the first (uncached) send of a prompt and a subsequent send eligible for cache hits.
phase2_kv_cache_ttft.note: v4's new PromptKVStore class is NOT wired into server.py's inference path - see PRECHECK.md. We measure the pre-existing '--disk-prompt-cache' flag which IS wired up and DOES integrate with the dispatch in server.py. That flag silently no-ops unless '--kv-cache-mode int8' is also passed; both flags are set together in the 'squish_kv' config.

phase3_spec_decode.description: Speculative decoding throughput vs baseline (7B INT4 target + 1.5B INT4 draft).
phase3_spec_decode.status: NOT MEASURED - squish.server crashes on --draft-model with ImportError: cannot import name 'load_draft_model' from 'squish.speculative'
phase3_spec_decode.what_was_skipped_reason: The v4 PR added --draft-model and --no-spec flags but 'load_draft_model' exists in squish/speculative/speculative.py and is not re-exported from squish/speculative/__init__.py. A one-line __init__.py patch would unblock the path. Per scope guards we do not fix v4 implementation bugs in this benchmarking session.

phase4_revisit_baseline_metrics.description: Peak RAM, disk size, and warm tok/s rerun under the same protocol as v3, with the new 'squish_kv' config included.
phase4_revisit_baseline_metrics.rationale: Establish whether int8 KV mode (required for the disk cache) changes the steady-state RAM or throughput vs v3's fp16 KV defaults.

phase5_results_update.description: Replace projected v4 numbers in docs/RESULTS.md and benchmarks/ollama_vs_squish/RESULTS.md with measured medians.

honest_caveats: v4 ships several flags ('--daemon', '--no-spec', '--no-cache') that are present in argparse but ignored by their dispatchers. The benchmark uses what actually executes.

We measure 'python -m squish.server' directly, not the user-facing 'squish run' CLI wrapper. The CLI delegates to the same server but does not expose '--disk-prompt-cache'. For a like-for-like apples-to-apples user comparison the CLI would need new flag plumbing.
The v3 result 'Squish (eager) cold-wall approx 7 s' is the cold-load baseline this benchmark explicitly avoids - daemon mode removes load cost from the user-visible wall.
Squish int8 KV mode is required for the disk cache to populate; this changes the steady-state RAM footprint vs fp16, which is also reported here.

reproduce.daemon_mode_via_cli: squish daemon start ~/models/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-int4   # HTTP-based daemon; squishd UDS path is broken for mlx-native quant
reproduce.spec_decode: BLOCKED: ImportError: cannot import name 'load_draft_model' from 'squish.speculative' (see PRECHECK.md follow-up #1)
